word: G4105 - πλανάω - planáō - plan-ah'-o - Go astray

sections.paragraph:
- Original: πλανάω

- Transliteration: Planao

- Phonetic: plan-ah'-o

- Definition:

1. to cause to stray, to lead astray, lead aside from the right way

a. to go astray, wander, roam about

2. metaph.

a. to lead away from the truth, to lead into error, to deceive

b. to be led into error

c. to be led aside from the path of virtue, to go astray, sin

d. to sever or fall away from the truth

1. of heretics

e. to be led away into error and sin

- Origin: from G4106

- TDNT entry: 09:48,9

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G4106; to (properly cause to) roam (from safety truth or virtue): - go astray deceive err seduce wander be out of the way.
